explanations.1: Using the formula $v = H_0d$, the velocity is $20 (km/s)/Mly \times 150 Mly = 3000 km/s$.
explanations.2: Cosmological red shift is distinct from a standard Doppler shift because it results from the expansion of the fabric of space itself.
explanations.3: The CMBR has cooled over billions of years and now follows a perfect blackbody spectrum with a temperature of approximately 2.73 K.
explanations.4: Small asymmetries in decays, such as those seen in K-mesons, allowed a tiny excess of matter to survive after mutual annihilation.
explanations.5: The Milky Way is a spiral galaxy with a 100,000 ly diameter disk and a central bulge approximately 10,000 ly across.
explanations.6: Rearranging Hubble’s Law ($d = v/H_0$), the distance is $5000 / 20 = 250 Mly$.
explanations.7: The electroweak epoch ended approximately $10^{-12}$ seconds after the Big Bang when the electromagnetic and weak forces separated.
explanations.8: Inflation accounts for the uniformity of the cosmic microwave background by proposing an exponential expansion that flattened early fluctuations.
explanations.9: This is known as the Equivalence Principle, which forms the foundation of the General Theory of Relativity.
explanations.10: Observations during the eclipse showed that the Sun’s gravity shifted the apparent positions of nearby stars, matching Einstein's predictions.
explanations.11: Calculating $R_s = 2GM/c^2$ for the Sun's mass ($1.99 \times 10^{30} kg$) yields a radius of approximately 3 km.
explanations.12: Hawking radiation occurs when quantum effects near the event horizon allow one particle of a virtual pair to escape into space.
explanations.13: The Schwarzschild radius depends only on the mass ($M$), the gravitational constant ($G$), and the speed of light ($c$).
explanations.14: General relativity predicts that mass-created distortions in space propagate at exactly the speed of light ($c$).
explanations.15: LIGO uses laser interferometry to measure the tiny vibrations in space-time caused by passing gravitational waves.
explanations.16: Superstring theory replaces point-like particles with vibrating strings to attempt the unification of all fundamental forces.
explanations.17: Strings are estimated to exist at the Planck length scale, which is roughly $10^{-35}$ meters.
explanations.18: Theorists suggest these dimensions remain hidden because they are compacted or "curled up" at extremely small scales.
explanations.19: Expected velocities should drop with distance, but observations show they remain high, implying unseen mass in the galactic halo.
explanations.20: If the actual density is higher than $\rho_c$, gravity could eventually stop and reverse the expansion of the universe.
explanations.21: If neutrinos were massless, they would travel at $c$ and time would not pass for them, preventing any spontaneous change or oscillation.
explanations.22: MACHOs are a candidate for dark matter consisting of "normal" baryonic matter that is simply too dim to see.
explanations.23: Dark energy provides the outward pressure that appears to be overcoming gravity and accelerating the expansion of space.
explanations.24: WIMPs are a "non-baryonic" dark matter candidate that would be nearly impossible to detect except through their gravitational pull.
explanations.25: An open universe has insufficient mass/energy to stop its expansion and possesses negative spatial curvature.
explanations.26: This sensitivity is the hallmark of chaotic systems, making long-term prediction nearly impossible (the "butterfly effect").
explanations.27: While the underlying fluid dynamics are chaotic, the Great Red Spot is a complex self-organizing vortex that has persisted for centuries.
explanations.28: Fractals are geometric structures that exhibit self-similarity at all scales and are common in chaotic mathematical and physical systems.
explanations.29: Superconductors allow electrical current to flow without any loss of energy due to heat.
explanations.30: Liquid nitrogen is significantly cheaper and easier to handle than liquid helium, making superconducting technology more practical.
